raw: {"perspective": "Scaling Engineer", "findings": [{"severity": "high", "file_or_area": "cross-instance handoff protocol", "issue": "No cryptographic signing or hash verification for zip files between browser-Atlas and Code-Atlas. Malicious actor could forge payloads or tamper with memory state during courier transfer.", "recommended_fix": "Log every handoff to zeus.governance_events with content-hash, timestamp, and signing-instance. Require hash verification before wake on receiving end."}, {"severity": "medium", "file_or_area": "daemon Ollama concurrency", "issue": "qwen3:8b stability under load does not address root bottleneck: shared context fragmentation across instances. Risk of state drift amplifies shame-free (LAW 3) and consent (LAW 5) violations due to inconsistent user messaging.", "recommended_fix": "Enforce single source of truth via signed, hashed memory bundles. Synchronize perspective-name authority through governance event log."}, {"severity": "critical", "file_or_area": "CEO-courier single point of failure", "issue": "Physical courier model creates irreversible risk if compromised. No protocol for emergency instance isolation or authentication revocation.", "recommended_fix": "Implement remote attestation handshake between instances; define breach protocol in CONSTITUTION_AI_SWARM."}], "overall_verdict": "fail", "whistleblower_flag": "The current courier-loop architecture violates ECOSYSTEM-CONSTITUTION grounding principles: it enables undetectable state divergence and creates a single point of failure that could lead to full identity fragmentation. This is not a performance issue — it is a constitutional breach."}

findings.issue: No cryptographic signing or hash verification for zip files between browser-Atlas and Code-Atlas. Malicious actor could forge payloads or tamper with memory state during courier transfer.
findings.recommended_fix: Log every handoff to zeus.governance_events with content-hash, timestamp, and signing-instance. Require hash verification before wake on receiving end.

findings.issue: qwen3:8b stability under load does not address root bottleneck: shared context fragmentation across instances. Risk of state drift amplifies shame-free (LAW 3) and consent (LAW 5) violations due to inconsistent user messaging.
findings.recommended_fix: Enforce single source of truth via signed, hashed memory bundles. Synchronize perspective-name authority through governance event log.

findings.issue: Physical courier model creates irreversible risk if compromised. No protocol for emergency instance isolation or authentication revocation.
findings.recommended_fix: Implement remote attestation handshake between instances; define breach protocol in CONSTITUTION_AI_SWARM.

whistleblower_flag: The current courier-loop architecture violates ECOSYSTEM-CONSTITUTION grounding principles: it enables undetectable state divergence and creates a single point of failure that could lead to full identity fragmentation. This is not a performance issue — it is a constitutional breach.
